properties.remark: Starting at 11:15 AM, the precipitation sensor was detected and live webcams showed precipitation increasing from the west. Road surface temperatures were recorded to be between 25 degrees at the start and 23 degrees by 12:30 PM. Wind gusts were between 10 mph and 23 mph from 11:15 AM to 12:30 PM. MDT 15 minute webcam imagery showed near zero visibility between 12 PM and 1 PM. Precipitation sensor stopped reporting activity at 12:45 PM and webcam imagery showed visibility improvement after 1 PM MST.

Light snow started at the airport at 11:30 AM. Visibility quickly reduced down to 0.75 miles in 9 minutes. ASOS reported winds gusting between 35 and 39 mph. Moderate snow fall by the ASOS was reported between 11:45 and 11:55 AM with visibility down to 0.5 miles. Visibility improved to over 1 mile after 11:55 AM. Airport staff called at 11:45 AM with 0.8 inches of new snow at the airport from the snow squall that passed through and near zero visibility. FAA Webcam 15 minute imagery showed the reduction in visibility of less than a quarter of a mile.

At 11:35 AM, Three Forks AWOS reported wind speeds sustained at 40 mph and gusts at 52 mph. These conditions continued until 12:15 PM. METAR observation at 12:55 PM reported visibility down to 0.25 miles and haze conditions. FAA webcam imagery between 11:45 AM and 12:30 PM showed snow falling with less than a quarter mile of visibility. Visibility improved to 7 miles at the 1:55 PM METAR observation.

Societal Impacts: Numerous flights (both private and commercial) were diverted or cancelled for Bozeman Airport that had estimated time for arrival between 1 PM and 3 PM. A time lapse video was shared on Facebook by a member of the public at the airport of when the snow squall moved through the airport.
Snow Squall. Visibility less than a quarter of a mile. Heavy snowfall. Blizzard conditions reported by the spotter.

Corrects previous snow squall report from 1 NE Belgrade. At 1:20 PM, Bozeman Airport ASOS reported light snow and 40 mph wind gusts with visibility down to 1.25 miles. 4 minutes later, ASOS reported wind gusts up to 44 mph and visibility down to 0.25 miles. These conditions continued for 10 minutes before visibility improved to 0.5 miles, but gusty winds between 30 mph and 45 mph continued for 1 hour. ASOS reported heavy snowfall for 5 minutes, then moderate snow for 10 minutes, then light snow for 20 minutes.
Snow Squall. Visibility down to a quarter of a mile. Heavy snowfall. Winds gusting near 44 mph.

Societal Impacts: Live webcams across Bozeman City showed when the snow squall went through the city between 1:30 PM and 2:30 PM MST with near zero visibility. The Logan Landfill reported 25 to 30 mph wind gusts with blizzard like conditions during this time frame from a Facebook post shared by Gallatin Solid Waste Management District. We received two mPing reports from the public with the tag snow squall and detailed of reduced visibility between 1:30 PM and 2:30 PM MST.
Snow and blowing snow reducing visibility to a quarter mile.

Corrects previous snow squall report from Church Hill. A trained spotter reported blizzard like conditions beginning at 1:16 PM. As of 1:36 PM, visibility had been reduced to under one-quarter of a mile. Spotter also reported blowing and drifting snow with no measured wind gusts.
Heavy snow with visibility down to 1/8 of a mile.

Delayed report - A small convective shower moved over Bozeman, which resulted in a heat burst in town. Broadcast media reported tree damage in Bozeman associated with this event. An Ambient weather station on Sourdough Ridge Rd measured the wind gust being reported. Nearby stations reported a rise in temperature by 9 degrees over 5 minutes.
